---
title: "Generative Production"
subtitle: "A Study Guide for Filmmakers Learning Generative AI"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-depth: 2
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    documentclass: scrbook
    papersize: letter
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
  epub:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

# Introduction {.unnumbered}

This is a single-document version of the Generative Production curriculum — foundations, generative media, vibe-coding for filmmakers, and the practical matters that sit under both. It is assembled from the same markdown source that powers the website at [generative-production.com](https://generative-production.com). Read straight through for a complete pass, or jump to any section via the table of contents.

A note on the reading order: this bundle is flat on purpose. Each chapter below is the same short explainer you would find on a topic page of the site, in the order a teacher would assign them. Internal cross-references use the site URLs, so some links will take you out of this file to the web version.

---

*Part I — Foundations. The umbrella claim: media generation and code generation are the same discipline. The following four chapters earn that claim.*

{{< include foundations/what-is-generative-production.md >}}

{{< include foundations/prompting-principles.md >}}

{{< include foundations/iteration-and-selection.md >}}

{{< include foundations/tracking-your-work.md >}}

---

*Part II — Generative Media. Pages on producing images, video, audio, and spatial work with generative tools.*

{{< include generative-media/concepts/index.md >}}

{{< include generative-media/concepts/model-basics/index.md >}}

{{< include generative-media/concepts/model-basics/seeds.md >}}

{{< include generative-media/concepts/modalities/index.md >}}

{{< include generative-media/concepts/tools-and-vendors/index.md >}}

{{< include generative-media/craft/index.md >}}

{{< include generative-media/lifecycle/index.md >}}

---

*Part III — Vibe-coding for Filmmakers. Writing code with AI coding tools, treated as a first-class form of generative production.*

{{< include vibe-coding/foundations/index.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/setup-and-safety/index.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/working-with-models/index.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/building-software/index.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/index.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/committing-secrets.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/blindly-running-commands.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/force-push-disasters.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/refactor-without-review.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/first-thing-that-compiles.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/scope-creep-prompts.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/building-on-sand.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/runaway-api-bills.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/deploy-without-testing.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/local-model-demo-trap.md >}}

{{< include vibe-coding/common-traps/works-on-my-machine.md >}}

---

*Part IV — Practical. The non-creative matters that sit under both pillars: licenses, cost, reproducibility, provenance, delivery.*

{{< include practical/index.md >}}

---

{{< include glossary.md >}}

{{< include learning-sources/index.md >}}
